ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR DEFENDANT QUENTIN COTILLARD, AN INDIVIDUAL NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 15.1 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (PART 2 OF 4 TENATIVE RULING) HEARING SET FOR JUL-19-2024 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT QUENTIN COTILLARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 15.1 AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED IN PART, GRANTED IN PART. SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,100.00, HALF OF THE REQUESTED AMOUNT REFLECTING HALF OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL BEING GRANTED AND HALF DENIED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT AND LOOMIS JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY AND ARE PAYABLE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THE DATE OF THE ORDER MEMORIALIZING THESE RULINGS. COUNSEL FOR COTILLARD IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLAINCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM PHIULIP WARD AT PSW@HASSARD.COM. [SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETIALS] JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP WARD; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT) ***THE COURT NOTES THAT THIS MATTER WAS CONTINUED PER A COURT ORDER FILED ON JULY 16, 2024 THEREFORE THE COURT VACATES THE COURT'S RULING AND CONTINUES THIS MATTER TO SEPTEMBER 9, 2024 PER COURT ORDER***

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatory No. 15.1 And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT QUENTIN COTILLARD'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatory No. 15.1 And Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED in part, GRANTED in part. Sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $2,100.00, half of the requested amount reflecting half of the motion to compel being granted and half denied. Sanctions are awarded against cross-defendant and Loomis jointly and severally and are payable within 15 days of the date of the order memorializing these rulings.

Counsel for is directed to prepare a form of proposed order consistent with the foregoing and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Counsel for Cotillard is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Phiulip Ward at psw@hassard.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
